name: Humayd ibn Muslim
title: The Chronicler of the Event
role: Reporter in Umar ibn Sa'd's Army
shortDescription: He was a soldier in Umar ibn Sa'd's army. Most of the details of Ashura have entered history through his reports.
fullStory: Humayd ibn Muslim was a member of Umar ibn Sa'd's army present at Karbala on Ashura, though he did not actively participate in the combat. He was primarily an observer and is recognized as one of the most critical historical sources of the Battle of Karbala. In his history, al-Tabari recounts many precise details of Ashura and the martyrdom of Imam Hussain's companions through Humayd's narrations. He also reportedly intervened to prevent Shimr from killing Imam Sajjad (AS).
quoteArabic: فوالله ما رأيت مكثوراً قط، قد قُتل ولده وأهل بيته وأصحابه، أربط جأشاً منه.
quote: By Allah, I have never seen anyone surrounded, whose children and companions were killed, fight so bravely and resolutely.
